text.title: Reflexion Memory Architecture
text.summary: Structure and update rules for the four memory files in .aidocs/memory/.

rule.statement: Maintain exactly four memory files in `.aidocs/memory/`: patterns.md, mistakes.md, decisions.md, session.md. Keep them in the same repository as the code they govern.
rule.rationale: Memory files in a different repo from the code produce context drift — patterns reference files and patterns that no longer exist in the agent's working tree.

rule.statement: A PAT-NNN entry must include: domain, context (when it applies), problem it solves, solution (actionable steps or code), confidence score, usage count, and source task ID.
rule.rationale: Patterns without context cannot be retrieved accurately. Patterns without confidence scores cannot be prioritized by the agent during Plan phase loading.

rule.statement: Write patterns in "When X, do Y" format, not as descriptions of what worked. "When migrating tables with >100k rows, batch the update" not "batching worked well".
rule.rationale: Prescriptive patterns are directly actionable. Descriptive patterns require the agent to re-derive the action, introducing drift.

rule.statement: Clear session.md at the start of each new project. Session context from a previous project causes the agent to apply irrelevant patterns and miss-attribute mistakes.
rule.rationale: Session.md is project-scoped. Multi-agent pipelines sharing session.md across projects produce write conflicts and corrupted context.

rule.statement: Archive patterns with confidence below 0.3 or unused for 90+ days into patterns-archive.md. Do not delete — archived patterns may be relevant again if the domain returns.
rule.rationale: Stale patterns loaded in context introduce noise. High-scoring but untouched patterns may have been invalidated by framework upgrades or architectural changes.

antipattern.description: Loading the full patterns.md without keyword filtering before a task.
antipattern.reason: A mature project's patterns.md can reach 15-20k tokens. Full loads exceed the practical context budget before the task description is even added.
